metadata.title: Operation Dust Storm Energy Sector Cyber Attack Scenario

Scenario.overview: Energy Sector Complex Operation Dust Storm targets the energy sector with a sophisticated cyber attack plan involving multiple stages and techniques.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets Cloud Instance OS Process Windows Registry File System Network Traffic Logon Session System Image
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Delivery Method Process Creation (Mechanism) Command Execution (Mechanism) Windows Registry Key Creation (Mechanism) OS API Execution (Mechanism) Network Traffic Flow (Mechanism) Logon Session Creation (Mechanism) File Deletion Steganography Network Traffic Content (Mechanism)

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attacker uses artificial intelligence to gather information about the target artifact.

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attacker uses Reg.exe utility to sniff network traffic related to credential stores.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.capec: CAPEC-670: Software Development Tools Maliciously Altered
stage.description: The attacker creates a cloud instance using compromised software deployment tools.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2025-24985 (Suggested): Microsoft Windows Fast FAT File System Driver contains an integer overflow or wraparound vulnerability that allows an unauthorized attacker to execute code locally.
stage.description: The attacker sends a spear phishing link to the target via internet-facing assets.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2021-41357 (Suggested): Microsoft Win32k contains an unspecified vulnerability that allows for privilege escalation.
stage.description: The attacker uses drive-by download to compromise the target via internet-facing assets.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2023-6448 (Suggested): Unitronics Vision Series PLCs and HMIs ship with an insecure default password, which if left unchanged, can allow attackers to execute remote commands.
stage.capec: CAPEC-663: Exploitation of Transient Instruction Execution
stage.description: The attacker executes malicious code on the target command line.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2016-10174 (Suggested): The NETGEAR WNR2000v5 router contains a buffer overflow which can be exploited to achieve remote code execution.
stage.description: The attacker schedules a task in the Windows registry.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: The attacker maintains persistence by scheduling tasks in the Windows registry.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: The attacker escalates privileges by exploiting a public-facing application.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.capec: CAPEC-702: Exploiting Incorrect Chaining or Granularity of Hardware Debug Components
stage.description: The attacker evades detection by creating processes.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.description: The attacker captures credentials via keylogging.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.capec: CAPEC-643: Identify Shared Files/Directories on System
stage.description: The attacker discovers network shares via the file system.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.description: The attacker moves laterally via network traffic.

stage.name: Collection
stage.description: The attacker collects emails via logon sessions.

stage.name: Collection
stage.capec: CAPEC-149: Explore for Predictable Temporary File Names
stage.description: The attacker deletes files via the file system.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.capec: CAPEC-636: Hiding Malicious Data or Code within Files
stage.description: The attacker exfiltrates data via steganography.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attacker causes denial-of-service via network traffic.

Scenario.summary: Operation Dust Storm is a complex cyber attack targeting the energy sector with multiple stages and techniques.
